stance: Risk-aware value, tilting cash into cheap names where analyst revisions are turning up; pruning the refuted, low-conviction tail.
experience_note: This is round one, so I am pre-registering rather than reacting. I am watching whether the high-revision value names I am adding (ONGC, LICI, GUJGASLTD, CHENNPETRO, MUTHOOTFIN, COFORGE, GRASIM) actually outperform the falling-revision tail I am cutting (MGL, EMAMI, AMBUJACEM, PRAJIND, CYIENT, PCBL) over the next 1-3 months. What would change my mind: if the names I trimmed for weak revisions rebound HARDER than the ones I added, that refutes the value_revision tilt and I should lean more on raw cheapness (z_val) and less on revision momentum. I am also watching my conviction-IC: did I size the eventual winners bigger? If my biggest-weight adds underperform my smaller ones, my sizing is noise and I should flatten toward equal-weight. Because z_val dispersion in this candidate set is thin (most names cluster near peer-average cheapness) while revision dispersion is wide, I expect this round's active return to be driven mostly by the revision tilt — if it fails, that is the lesson.
